content.text:
<timestamp>Saturday, Jul 18, 2026, 12:19 AM (UTC+4)</timestamp>

i just deployed the website and the panel is down now.
find the problem on aws.
I'll dig into the AWS panel deployment — starting with ops docs and how we check the EC2 app health.
Checking container status and whether kuchup.com responds.

arguments.command: curl -sI --max-time 10 https://kuchup.com 2>&1; echo '---'; curl -sI --max-time 10 https://kuchup.com/api/auth/status 2>&1; echo '---'; curl -sf --max-time 10 https://kuchup.com/api/auth/status 2>&1; echo "exit: $?"

content.text: Panel is crash-looping (`Restarting`). Pulling its logs and checking Postgres.

arguments.command: source aws-postgres.env && ssh -i ~/Downloads/relocation.pem -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new ec2-user@${ELASTIC_IP} 'echo "=== docker ps -a ==="; docker ps -a --format "table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}\t{{.Ports}}"; echo; echo "=== panel logs ==="; docker logs relocation-panel --tail 80 2>&1; echo; echo "=== disk ==="; df -h /; echo; echo "=== free mem ==="; free -h'

arguments.description: Fetch panel crash logs and host health

content.text: Root cause: panel crash-loops on `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'playwright'` — the slim EC2 image excludes Playwright, but boot now imports it eagerly. Tracing the import chain and the intended fix.

arguments.command: rg -n "PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE|playwright" relocation_jobs/core/ats_detection.py | head -40; echo '---'; rg -n "from playwright|import playwright|scrape_board_with_playwright" relocation_jobs/ --glob '*.py'

content.text: Admin only needs scheduler config helpers but pulls the whole scrape chain. Checking whether we can cut that import without a bigger redesign.
Panel boots the scrape graph; three modules import Playwright unconditionally while the slim EC2 image omits it. Making those imports optional (same pattern as `ats_detection`) and redeploying.

arguments.old_string:
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

from relocation_jobs.core.ats_detection import (
    PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE,
    _playwright_browser_context,
    _playwright_pause,
    _playwright_sem,
)

    PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE,
    _playwright_browser_context,
    _playwright_pause,
    _playwright_sem,
)
from relocation_jobs.core.scrape_cancel import FetchCancelled, raise_if_cancelled
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

from __future__ import annotations

from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

from relocation_jobs.core.ats_detection import (
    PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE,
    _playwright_browser_context,
    _playwright_pause,
    _playwright_sem,

arguments.command:
python3 -c "
import sys
sys.modules['playwright'] = None  # force missing
# better: just import without playwright installed
from relocation_jobs.web.server import app
print('app ok', app)
" 2>&1 | tail -30
python3 -c "from relocation_jobs.web.server import app; print('app ok')" 2>&1 | tail -40

python3 -c "
import sys, builtins
# Simulate panel image: playwright not installed
import importlib
real_import = builtins.__import__
def fake_import(name, *a, **kw):
    if name == 'playwright' or name.startswith('playwright.'):
        raise ImportError('No module named playwright')
    return real_import(name, *a, **kw)
builtins.__import__ = fake_import
# Clear any cached playwright modules
for k in list(sys.modules):
    if k == 'playwright' or k.startswith('playwright.') or k.startswith('relocation_jobs'):
        del sys.modules[k]
from relocation_jobs.web.server import app
from relocation_jobs.core.ats_detection import PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE
print('app ok', 'PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE=', PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE)
" 2>&1

content.text: Import works without Playwright. Redeploying the panel to EC2.
Skipping a full worker rebuild — syncing and rebuilding only the panel image.

arguments.command:
source aws-postgres.env
REMOTE_DIR=/home/ec2-user/relocation-jobs
PANEL_IMAGE=relocation-panel:ec2
PANEL_CONTAINER=relocation-panel
SSH=(ssh -i ~/Downloads/relocation.pem -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new ec2-user@${ELASTIC_IP})

echo "=== prune dangling images ==="
"${SSH[@]}" 'docker image prune -f; df -h / | awk "NR==2"'

echo "=== build panel ==="
"${SSH[@]}" "cd ${REMOTE_DIR} && DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --build-arg BUILDKIT_INLINE_CACHE=1 --cache-from ${PANEL_IMAGE} -f Dockerfile.ec2 -t ${PANEL_IMAGE} ."

echo "=== recreate panel with prior env ==="
"${SSH[@]}" bash -s <<'EOF'
set -euo pipefail
# Capture env from crash-looping container (Config.Env still present)
mapfile -t ENV_ARGS < <(docker inspect relocation-panel --format '{{range .Config.Env}}-e{{println .}}{{end}}' | sed '/^$/d')
PORT_BIND=$(docker inspect relocation-panel --format '{{range \$p, \$conf := .HostConfig.PortBindings}}{{(index \$conf 0).HostPort}}:{{(index (split \$p "/") 0)}}{{end}}')
# Fallback if empty
if [[ -z "$PORT_BIND" ]]; then PORT_BIND=10000:10000; fi
docker rm -f relocation-panel
docker run -d --name relocation-panel --restart unless-stopped \
  -p "$PORT_BIND" \
  -v /home/ec2-user/relocation-jobs/relocation_jobs/static:/app/relocation_jobs/static:ro \
  "${ENV_ARGS[@]}" \
  relocation-panel:ec2
sleep 3
docker ps --filter name=relocation-panel --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}'
echo '--- logs ---'
docker logs relocation-panel --tail 30 2>&1
echo '--- health ---'
curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:10000/api/auth/status
EOF
arguments.description: Rebuild and restart panel container only

content.text: Build succeeded. Restarting the panel with the deploy script's env pattern.

arguments.command:
source aws-postgres.env
# Pull redis password and panel secrets the same way deploy does
REDIS_PASS=""
if [[ -n "${REDIS_PASSWORD:-}" ]]; then REDIS_PASS="$REDIS_PASSWORD"; else
  url=$(grep -E '^REDIS_URL=' .env | cut -d= -f2- || true)
  if [[ "$url" =~ REDACTED ]]; then REDIS_PASS="${BASH_REMATCH[1]}"; fi
fi
SECRET=$(grep -E '^PANEL_SECRET_KEY=' .env | cut -d= -f2-)
ADMIN_PASS=$(grep -E '^PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD=' .env | cut -d= -f2-)
DB_URL="REDACTED"
REDIS_URL="REDACTED"

ssh -i ~/Downloads/relocation.pem -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new ec2-user@${ELASTIC_IP} bash -s <<EOF
set -euo pipefail
docker rm -f relocation-panel 2>/dev/null || true
docker run -d --name relocation-panel --restart unless-stopped \\
  -p 10000:10000 \\
  -v /home/ec2-user/relocation-jobs/relocation_jobs/static:/app/relocation_jobs/static:ro \\
  -e PORT=10000 \\
  -e PANEL_SCRAPE_ENABLED=0 \\
  -e PANEL_COMPANY_FETCH_ENABLED=1 \\
  -e PANEL_DATA_DIR=/tmp/panel-data \\
  -e PANEL_SECRET_KEY='${SECRET}' \\
  -e PANEL_ADMIN_USER=admin \\
  -e PANEL_ADMIN_PASSWORD='${ADMIN_PASS}' \\
  -e PANEL_ALLOW_REGISTER=0 \\
  -e FETCH_SCHEDULE_ENABLED=1 \\
  -e FETCH_SCHEDULE_INTERVAL_HOURS=6 \\
  -e FETCH_SCHEDULE_CONCURRENCY=4 \\
  -e DATABASE_URL='${DB_URL}' \\
  -e REDIS_URL='${REDIS_URL}' \\
  relocation-panel:ec2
sleep 4
docker ps --filter name=relocation-panel --format 'table {{.Names}}\t{{.Status}}'
echo '--- logs ---'
docker logs relocation-panel --tail 40 2>&1
echo '--- health ---'
curl -s -o /dev/null -w 'local:%{http_code}\n' --max-time 10 http://127.0.0.1:10000/api/auth/status
EOF
echo "--- domain ---"
curl -s -o /dev/null -w 'https://kuchup.com/api/auth/status -> %{http_code}\n' --max-time 15 https://kuchup.com/api/auth/status

content.text:
Panel is back up (`https://kuchup.com/api/auth/status` → 200).

**Cause:** The slim EC2 panel image intentionally has no Playwright. After the deploy, gunicorn still imported the scrape stack at boot (`admin` → `fetch.scheduler` → … → `playwright_board`), which did `from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright` unconditionally → crash loop → 502.

**Fix:** Guard Playwright imports in `playwright_board.py`, `hibob.py`, and `playwright_ats.py` behind `PLAYWRIGHT_AVAILABLE` (same idea as `ats_detection`). Rebuilt and restarted only the panel container.

Worker was fine the whole time; Playwright scrapes still run there.
